policies.priceDropPolicy: Delta does not offer direct price-drop refunds. If the price drops after booking, customers can cancel and rebook (paying applicable fees) or use eCredit from cancelled ticket toward the new booking. For refundable fares, cancel and rebook at lower price for full cash refund.
policies.twentyFourHourRule: Full refund within 24 hours of purchase for tickets originating in the US, regardless of fare type. Cancel via My Trips at delta.com. Applies to eTickets only.
policies.basicEconomyRules: Delta Main Basic (Basic Economy) and Delta Comfort Basic: Not eligible for Same-Day Standby or Same-Day Confirmed; cancellation charge $99-$400 depending on route; change fees $0-$400 plus price difference. Check Baggage & Travel Fees page for specifics by origin/destination.
policies.awardTicketPolicy: SkyMiles award tickets: miles redeposited to SkyMiles account upon cancellation. Redeposit fees may apply depending on Medallion status. Diamond/Platinum Medallion members: free redeposit. Lower tiers may incur fees. Taxes/fees refunded to original form of payment.
claimSteps: Step 1: Log in to delta.com and navigate to My Trips
Step 2: Select the booking you wish to cancel or change
Step 3: Choose 'Change Flight' (to rebook at lower fare) or 'Cancel Flight'
Step 4: If cancelling a non-refundable ticket, remaining value (minus cancellation fee) issues as an eCredit
Step 5: For refundable tickets, click 'Request Refund' at delta.com/refund-form
Step 6: Rebook at the new lower price using eCredit or pay difference
policies.creditExpiry: eCredits typically valid 1 year from original ticket issue date. If issued from cancelled ticket, expiry tied to original ticket date.

cancellationFees.classicNonRefundable: $0–$400 depending on route; $0 for US/Canada-originating itineraries since elimination of change fees
cancellationFees.sameDayConfirmed: From $75 for Classic; free for Extra/Refundable when available

refundTypes.miles: SkyMiles redeposited for award cancellations (fees may apply)

loopholes: No change fees for Classic/Extra tickets originating US/Canada — rebook at lower price and keep difference as eCredit
If Delta changes your flight schedule by 30+ minutes, you qualify for a full cash refund regardless of fare type — call to invoke this
Use the 24-hour risk-free window immediately if you find a lower price after booking
Same-Day Confirmed ($75) can be used the day of travel to grab a lower-priced earlier/later flight on same route
Medallion status holders often get waived redeposit fees on SkyMiles awards — check status before assuming fees apply
eCredits can be applied to new bookings for any passenger if transferred — check transfer rules

policies.priceDropPolicy: Southwest has the most consumer-friendly price drop policy among US carriers. If the price drops after booking, cancel and rebook at the lower price online — no fees. Difference issued as travel credit (Wanna Get Away) or cash (Anytime/Business Select). No change or cancellation fees on any fare.
policies.twentyFourHourRule: Full refund to original payment within 24 hours for all fare types if booked at least 7 days before departure. Wanna Get Away fares cancelled after 24 hours receive travel funds (credits), not cash.
policies.basicEconomyRules: Southwest has no Basic Economy equivalent. Wanna Get Away (cheapest fare) has the most restrictions: travel funds are non-transferable and valid 12 months from purchase. Wanna Get Away Plus: travel funds are transferable. Both can be cancelled without fee.
policies.awardTicketPolicy: Rapid Rewards points are immediately refunded to account upon cancellation for all fare types, with no redeposit fees. Taxes/fees ($5.60 per segment) refunded to original form of payment.
claimSteps: Step 1: Go to southwest.com and click 'My Trips' or 'Change/Cancel'
Step 2: Look up your booking by confirmation number + first/last name
Step 3: Select the flight to cancel
Step 4: Cancel the existing booking — funds issued as travel credit (Wanna Get Away) or cash (Anytime/Business Select)
Step 5: Search for and book the new lower-priced flight
Step 6: Apply travel funds at checkout using the confirmation number and passenger name
policies.creditExpiry: Wanna Get Away travel funds: valid 12 months from original purchase date (not flight date). Wanna Get Away Plus: transferable to another Rapid Rewards member. Anytime/Business Select: full cash refund, no expiry concern.

cancellationFees.wannaGetAway: $0 cancellation fee — funds issued as non-transferable travel credit valid 12 months
cancellationFees.wannaGetAwayPlus: $0 cancellation fee — funds issued as transferable travel credit valid 12 months
cancellationFees.anytime: $0 cancellation fee — full refund to original payment
cancellationFees.businessSelect: $0 cancellation fee — full refund to original payment

refundTypes.cash: Anytime and Business Select fares only
refundTypes.travelCredit: Wanna Get Away and Wanna Get Away Plus fares; valid 12 months
refundTypes.points: Rapid Rewards points refunded immediately with no redeposit fee

loopholes: Zero change/cancel fees on all fares — always cancel and rebook if price drops even $1
Wanna Get Away Plus travel funds are transferable — can be gifted to another traveler
Travel funds from a cancelled Wanna Get Away can be applied to a new booking for ANY Southwest passenger, not just the original traveler (as long as you hold the confirmation number)
Book Anytime fare for full flexibility with cash refunds, then switch to Wanna Get Away if price drops significantly
Monitor southwest.com directly for price changes — no GDS fees or OTA markups
If Southwest cancels/significantly changes your flight, you get a full cash refund regardless of fare type

policies.priceDropPolicy: JetBlue does not offer direct price-drop refunds to original payment. If fare drops, cancel and rebook: fare difference issued as JetBlue travel credit (not cash). Blue Basic fares incur a per-person cancellation fee before credit is issued. Net benefit = price drop minus cancellation fee.
policies.twentyFourHourRule: Free cancellation within 24 hours of booking IF the booking was made 7 or more days before the scheduled departure date. The entire itinerary must be cancelled (not just one segment). Not applicable for JetBlue Vacations packages.
policies.basicEconomyRules: Blue Basic fare: per-person cancellation fee applies (~$100 domestic, ~$200 international) before travel credit is issued. No same-day changes. Not eligible for same-day standby at no charge. Must pay fee even within policy window (except 24-hour rule). Loophole: cancellation fee may exceed price drop savings — calculate net before claiming.
policies.awardTicketPolicy: TrueBlue points refunded immediately to TrueBlue account upon cancellation. No redeposit fees stated. Taxes/fees refunded per standard timeline.
claimSteps: Step 1: Go to jetblue.com/manage-trips and sign in or look up booking
Step 2: Select the trip you wish to cancel
Step 3: Click 'Cancel' and follow cancellation prompts
Step 4: Note the travel credit amount (issued immediately to JetBlue Travel Bank)
Step 5: Search for and rebook the same/similar flight at the new lower price
Step 6: Apply travel credit at checkout (automatically available in your Travel Bank)
policies.creditExpiry: JetBlue travel credits valid 12 months from original ticketing date. If travel credit is used and then that booking is cancelled, the credit retains the original expiration date — does not reset.

cancellationFees.blueBasic: ~$100 per person (domestic), ~$200 per person (international) — deducted from credit issued
cancellationFees.blue: $0 cancellation fee — full amount as travel credit
cancellationFees.bluePlus: $0 cancellation fee — full amount as travel credit
cancellationFees.blueExtra: $0 cancellation fee — full amount as travel credit or cash if refundable
cancellationFees.mint: $0 for non-refundable — travel credit; refundable Mint → cash

refundTypes.cash: Refundable fares only (cancel any time before departure); credit card refund 7-10 business days
refundTypes.travelCredit: Non-refundable fares (Blue, Blue Plus, Blue Extra non-refundable); valid 12 months; issued immediately
refundTypes.points: TrueBlue points refunded immediately to account
refundTypes.extras: Seats, bags, pets refunded to original payment in 7-10 business days

loopholes: Always calculate net savings for Blue Basic: price_drop_savings minus cancellation_fee — skip claim if negative
If JetBlue changes your flight schedule, you can cancel and get a full cash refund to original payment regardless of fare type
Book directly on jetblue.com (not OTAs) to retain full refund/change rights
JetBlue Best Fare Guarantee: if you find a lower JetBlue fare elsewhere, they may match it — check jetblue.com/customer-assurance/our-promise
Travel credit refunds for fare changes go to Travel Bank (not original payment) — this is stated policy, cannot be reversed once processed
Seats/bags/extras are always refunded to original payment, not travel credit — useful for partial recovery

policies.priceDropPolicy: Alaska Airlines eliminated change fees on most fares. If the price drops, cancel and rebook online. Non-Saver fares: remaining value issued as eCredit minus any applicable fees. Saver fares are non-changeable and non-refundable. For refundable fares, full cash refund available.
policies.twentyFourHourRule: Full refund within 24 hours of booking for all fare types if the flight is at least 24 hours away. Cancel via the app or alaskaair.com. Applies to tickets purchased directly from Alaska Airlines.
policies.basicEconomyRules: Saver fare (Alaska's equivalent of Basic Economy): non-changeable, non-refundable after 24-hour window. Cannot upgrade cabin. Cannot earn or redeem Mileage Plan miles on most Saver bookings. Changes/cancellations result in full forfeiture of fare value.
policies.awardTicketPolicy: Mileage Plan award cancellations: miles redeposited to account. Redeposit fee: $125 for non-elite members; waived for MVP, MVP Gold, and MVP Gold 75K members. Taxes/fees refunded to original payment.
claimSteps: Step 1: Log in to alaskaair.com and go to 'Manage Reservations'
Step 2: Find your booking and select 'Change or Cancel Flight'
Step 3: Choose to cancel the current booking
Step 4: Confirm cancellation — remaining value (minus fees if applicable) issued as eCredit
Step 5: Search for the same route at the new lower price
Step 6: At checkout, select 'Use eCredit' to apply credit toward new booking
policies.creditExpiry: eCredits valid 12 months from original ticket issue date. Cannot be extended. Non-transferable to other passengers.

cancellationFees.saverFare: 100% forfeiture after 24-hour window — no credit issued
cancellationFees.mainCabin: $0 cancellation fee — remaining value as eCredit valid 12 months
cancellationFees.firstClass: $0 cancellation fee — refundable tickets get cash, non-refundable get eCredit
cancellationFees.awardRedeposit: $125 for non-elite; free for MVP, MVP Gold, MVP Gold 75K

refundTypes.cash: Refundable (Flex) fare tickets; also when Alaska changes/cancels your flight
refundTypes.eCredit: Non-refundable Main Cabin cancellations; valid 12 months
refundTypes.miles: Mileage Plan miles redeposited (fee applies for non-status members)

loopholes: Alaska is part of oneworld alliance — if a partner (AA, BA, etc.) changes flight significantly, Alaska may honor a full refund
If Alaska changes your itinerary by 30+ minutes, you qualify for full cash refund on any fare including Saver
MVP status members get free award redeposit — worth considering if you hold status
Alaska's price match: not officially offered, but proactive re-pricing requests via chat have succeeded
Book via Alaska's app for easiest online change/cancel management
Main Cabin (non-Saver) has zero change fees — freely rebook at lower price and apply eCredit difference

policies.priceDropPolicy: United eliminated change fees for most fares originating in the US, Canada, Europe, and other regions. If the price drops, cancel the existing booking (receiving an eCredit) and rebook at the lower price. Basic Economy is the only fare that cannot be changed or refunded after the 24-hour window.
policies.twentyFourHourRule: Full refund within 24 hours of purchase for all fare types except MileagePlus award tickets. Must cancel within 24 hours and the flight must be at least 7 days away. Process via the United app, united.com, or by calling.
policies.basicEconomyRules: Basic Economy (N, O, Q fare classes): No changes permitted after 24-hour window. Cancellation results in full forfeiture. Cannot upgrade seat. Cannot select seats in advance (assigned at check-in). Cannot earn Premier Qualifying Points. No same-day changes.
policies.awardTicketPolicy: MileagePlus award cancellations: miles redeposited. Redeposit fee: $75–$100 for non-elite members; waived for Premier Silver and above. Close-in booking fee: $75 for non-elite if booking within 21 days. Taxes refunded to original payment.
claimSteps: Step 1: Go to united.com and log in; navigate to 'My Trips'
Step 2: Select the booking with the price drop
Step 3: Click 'Change Flight' to explore rebooking directly, or 'Cancel' to get eCredit
Step 4: If cancelling, confirm and receive eCredit (valid 12 months from ticket issue date)
Step 5: Search for the same route at the new lower price
Step 6: At checkout, select 'Use eCredit/Certificate' to apply credit toward new fare
policies.creditExpiry: United eCredits valid 12 months from ticket issue date (not travel date). Future Flight Credits for cancelled non-refundable tickets are non-transferable.

cancellationFees.economy: $0 change/cancel fee for US/Canada/Europe-originating tickets — eCredit issued
cancellationFees.premiumEconomy: $0 change/cancel fee — eCredit or cash (if refundable)
cancellationFees.business: $0 change/cancel fee — eCredit or cash (if refundable)
cancellationFees.awardRedeposit: $75–$100 for non-Premier; free for Premier Silver and above

refundTypes.cash: Refundable fare tickets only; refund request at refunds.united.com
refundTypes.eCredit: Non-refundable ticket cancellations; valid 12 months from issue
refundTypes.miles: MileagePlus miles redeposited (fees may apply for non-status)

loopholes: If United changes your flight by 25+ minutes, you can request a full cash refund on any fare including Basic Economy — call to invoke
United's 'change flight' tool lets you rebook at a lower price directly; difference issued as eCredit without needing full cancellation
Premier Silver and above: free award redeposit — time cancellations strategically if you have status
Refunds for significantly delayed/cancelled flights: DOT requires cash refund for flights cancelled by airline — do not accept eCredit without asking for cash first
United credit cards (Chase) often come with rebooking protections — check card benefits before paying change fees
Book directly on united.com (not OTAs) to retain full change/cancel rights and access eCredit system

policies.priceDropPolicy: American Airlines does not offer direct price-drop refunds. If the fare drops after booking, cancel and rebook at the lower price. Non-Basic fares: trip credit issued for cancelled amount, apply toward new booking. Basic Economy: no changes permitted; cancellation yields trip credit minus $99 fee.
policies.twentyFourHourRule: Full refund within 24 hours of booking for all fare types if the flight is at least 2 days (48 hours) away. Must be booked directly through AA (aa.com, app, or phone). Cancel online or via app within 24-hour window for automatic full refund.
policies.basicEconomyRules: Basic Economy (B fare class): No changes after 24-hour window. Cancellations after 24 hours: trip credit issued minus $99 cancellation fee. Cannot upgrade. Cannot select seats in advance on most routes. Cannot use same-day change. Most restrictive fare — avoid for flexible travel.
policies.awardTicketPolicy: AAdvantage award cancellations: miles redeposited to account. Redeposit fee: $150 for non-status members; waived for AAdvantage Gold, Platinum, Platinum Pro, and Executive Platinum members. Fee charged per-person. Taxes/fees refunded to original payment within 7 business days.
claimSteps: Step 1: Go to aa.com/reservations or open the American Airlines app
Step 2: Log in and find your booking under 'Your Trips'
Step 3: Select 'Change Trip' to rebook at new lower fare directly (price difference becomes trip credit)
Step 4: Alternatively, select 'Cancel Trip' — trip credit issued for ticket value (minus fees if Basic Economy)
Step 5: Search for the same route at the new lower price
Step 6: At checkout, apply trip credit by selecting 'Use Trip Credit' and entering the credit number
policies.creditExpiry: Trip Credits (formerly eVouchers): valid 12 months from issue date. AAdvantage miles redeposited after fee payment — no expiry on miles once redeposited.

cancellationFees.basicEconomy: $99 per person cancellation fee after 24-hour window; remaining value as trip credit
cancellationFees.mainCabin: $0 cancellation fee — full trip credit issued
cancellationFees.premiumEconomy: $0 cancellation fee — trip credit or cash if refundable
cancellationFees.business: $0 cancellation fee — trip credit or cash if refundable
cancellationFees.first: $0 cancellation fee — trip credit or cash if refundable
cancellationFees.awardRedeposit: $150 per person for non-status members; free for Gold status and above

refundTypes.cash: Fully refundable fare tickets; also when AA cancels/significantly delays your flight
refundTypes.tripCredit: Non-refundable ticket cancellations; valid 12 months from issue date; non-transferable
refundTypes.miles: AAdvantage miles redeposited upon award cancellation (fee applies for non-status)

loopholes: If American cancels your flight or delays it by 3+ hours (domestic) or 6+ hours (international), you qualify for a full cash refund under DOT rules — always ask for cash, not trip credit
AAdvantage status members (Gold+) get free award redeposit — worth timing cancellations around status earn thresholds
Trip credits can be used online without calling — go to aa.com/reservation/tripCreditSearch.do
The AA app 'Change Trip' feature can sometimes rebook at lower fare same-day and issue difference as credit without full cancellation
Basic Economy $99 fee: only worth claiming if price drop exceeds $99 per person; calculate net savings first
Book directly on aa.com — OTA bookings require the OTA to process changes, not AA directly

policies.priceDropPolicy: British Airways does not offer a formal price-drop refund or price-match guarantee. If the price drops after booking, the recommended approach is to cancel and rebook at the lower fare. Hand Baggage Only (HBO) fares: cancellation fee applies. Standard/Flexible fares: cancel and rebook with potential credit or refund depending on fare rules. Avios award tickets: cancel and redeposit miles, then rebook at lower Avios rate.
policies.twentyFourHourRule: For tickets purchased in the United States, British Airways offers a 24-hour 'risk-free' cancellation window for bookings made at least 7 days before departure. Full refund to original payment method. Cancel via Manage My Booking at ba.com or by calling BA reservations. EU/UK-originating bookings: 14-day cooling-off right applies for package holidays only; individual flights follow fare rules.
policies.basicEconomyRules: Hand Baggage Only (HBO) fare — BA's most restrictive economy fare: no hold baggage included, seats assigned at check-in, changes and cancellations subject to a fee (~£60-100 per person), no upgrade eligibility. After 24-hour window: cancellation yields partial Avios or travel credit minus fee. Not eligible for seat selection at booking. Avoid for flexible travel.
policies.awardTicketPolicy: British Airways Executive Club Avios awards: Redeposit fee approximately £35 per person for non-Silver/non-Gold members. Silver Tier: reduced or waived redeposit fees. Gold Tier: fees typically waived. Taxes and surcharges (often substantial on BA): refunded to original payment within 7 business days. Cancel at least 24 hours before departure to be eligible for redeposit.
claimSteps: Step 1: Go to ba.com and log in to your Executive Club account
Step 2: Navigate to 'Manage My Booking' and enter booking reference + last name
Step 3: Select the flight you wish to cancel
Step 4: For Standard/Flexible fares: cancel for full refund to original payment or travel voucher
Step 5: For HBO fares: cancel noting the applicable fee — net credit issued
Step 6: Search for the same route at the new lower price and rebook
Step 7: For refund requests: complete the refund form at ba.com/refunds
policies.creditExpiry: BA travel vouchers: typically valid 12 months from issue date. Future Travel Vouchers (issued during COVID era): extended policies may apply. Avios miles: no expiry as long as account remains active (one Avios transaction every 36 months required).

cancellationFees.handBaggageOnly: ~£60-100 (~$75-125 USD) per person cancellation fee; remaining value as travel credit
cancellationFees.standard: Varies by fare rules — typically £0-50 per person; credit or refund depending on fare class
cancellationFees.flexible: £0 cancellation fee — full refund to original payment
cancellationFees.aviosRedeposit: ~£35 per person for Blue/Bronze; reduced/waived for Silver/Gold Tier members

refundTypes.cash: Flexible and fully refundable fares; also when BA cancels or significantly delays your flight
refundTypes.travelVoucher: Standard non-refundable fare cancellations; valid 12 months from issue
refundTypes.avios: Award ticket cancellations — miles redeposited (fee applies for non-Silver/Gold)

loopholes: EU261 regulation: If your BA flight originates in the UK or EU and is cancelled or delayed 3+ hours, you're entitled to £220-520 compensation regardless of fare type
If BA significantly changes your itinerary (2+ hour change), you can request a full cash refund on any fare type — always ask for cash, not a voucher
BA 'Book with Confidence' policy (check current availability): may allow free date changes on some fare types
Avios award bookings: cancel well in advance (before departure) — no-show forfeits all Avios with no redeposit option
Check whether your ticket was booked via AMEX Platinum or high-tier travel card — many include trip protection covering price drops and cancellations
HBO fares: baggage fees are charged separately — if price drops enough, it may be worth paying the cancellation fee and rebooking with the savings
BA.com price drops at T-21 days or less sometimes trigger better revenue management fares — monitor using TripReclaim

policies.priceDropPolicy: Lufthansa does not offer a direct price-drop refund or price match policy. If the price drops after booking, options depend on fare type: Light fare: no changes or refunds permitted after booking (except 24-hour window for US tickets). Classic fare: changes allowed for a fee; cancel and rebook at lower price. Flex fare: fully changeable and refundable at any time.
policies.twentyFourHourRule: For tickets originating in the United States, Lufthansa honors the US DOT 24-hour cancellation rule: full refund within 24 hours of booking if departure is at least 7 days away. Process the cancellation via Manage Booking at lufthansa.com or by phone. EU-originating flights: governed by EU261 regulations (stronger passenger rights on cancellations and delays, but no formal 24h cancellation rule for individual tickets).
policies.basicEconomyRules: Economy Light fare — Lufthansa's most restrictive economy tier: one carry-on bag only (no checked bag), seat selection not included, no changes permitted after booking (except within 24-hour US DOT window), non-refundable. Cancellation after 24h results in full forfeiture. No upgrade eligibility. For any flexibility, book Economy Classic or higher.
policies.awardTicketPolicy: Miles & More award tickets: miles redeposited upon cancellation. Redeposit fee: approximately €50 per person for non-status members (Frequent Traveller, Senator, HON Circle members receive reduced or waived fees). Cancel at least 24 hours before departure. Taxes and surcharges refunded to original payment within 7 business days.
claimSteps: Step 1: Log in to lufthansa.com and go to 'My Bookings' or use Manage Booking
Step 2: Enter booking code and last name to access your reservation
Step 3: Select the flight and review cancellation options based on your fare type
Step 4: For Classic fares: choose 'Change Flight' to rebook at new lower fare, paying any applicable change fee
Step 5: For Light fares within 24h: cancel for full refund before the window closes
Step 6: For Flex fares: cancel for full refund, then rebook at the lower price
Step 7: Submit refund request at lufthansa.com/refund if cash refund is applicable
policies.creditExpiry: Lufthansa travel credits: typically valid 12 months from issue date. Miles & More miles: valid while account remains active (minimum activity required — check Miles & More terms for current validity period).

cancellationFees.economyLight: 100% forfeiture after 24-hour window for US tickets — no refund or credit
cancellationFees.economyClassic: Change fee approximately €50-200 depending on route; remaining value as credit toward new fare
cancellationFees.economyFlex: €0 cancellation fee — full refund to original payment
cancellationFees.businessLight: Varies by fare sub-class — typically €100-300 change fee
cancellationFees.businessFlex: €0 cancellation fee — full refund to original payment
cancellationFees.milesRedeposit: ~€50 per person for non-status; reduced/waived for Frequent Traveller status and above

refundTypes.cash: Economy Flex and Business Flex fares; also mandated by EU261 when Lufthansa cancels or significantly delays
refundTypes.travelCredit: Classic fare cancellations (after deducting change fee); valid 12 months
refundTypes.miles: Miles & More award cancellations — miles redeposited (fee applies for non-status)

loopholes: EU261 Regulation: If your flight originates in Germany or any EU country and is cancelled or delayed 3+ hours, you're entitled to €250-600 compensation regardless of fare type — this is a legal right, not a policy
If Lufthansa cancels your flight or changes it by more than 5 hours, you can request a full cash refund on any fare including Economy Light
Lufthansa Group codeshares: your ticket may be operated by SWISS, Austrian, or Brussels Airlines — EU261 rights still apply for EU-origin flights
Economy Classic change fee is often worth paying if the price drop exceeds the fee — calculate net savings first
Lufthansa HON Circle, Senator, and Frequent Traveller members get significant fee waivers — check your status before assuming full fees apply
Book directly at lufthansa.com — OTA bookings complicate change/cancel requests significantly

policies.priceDropPolicy: Qatar Airways does not offer a formal price-drop refund or price-match guarantee. Options after a price drop depend on fare class: Economy Lite: non-refundable, no changes; full forfeiture except within 24-hour US DOT window. Economy Classic: changes permitted with a fee. Economy Flex: fully changeable and refundable. Business: typically refundable. For non-refundable fares, cancellation plus rebooking at the lower price is the primary strategy.
policies.twentyFourHourRule: For tickets originating in the United States, Qatar Airways complies with US DOT 24-hour cancellation rules: full refund within 24 hours of purchase if the departure date is at least 7 days away. Cancel online via Manage Booking or by calling Qatar Airways US reservations. EU-originating Qatar flights: EU261 applies for flights operating on the EU leg.
policies.basicEconomyRules: Economy Lite — Qatar's most restrictive economy fare: one carry-on bag (no checked baggage unless purchased separately), seat selection not included, no changes or date modifications after booking, non-refundable after the 24-hour window. Cancellation results in full forfeiture of ticket value. Not eligible for complimentary upgrades. For any flexibility, book Economy Classic or higher.
policies.awardTicketPolicy: Privilege Club Avios (Qatar Airways loyalty program merged with British Airways Avios in 2021): award tickets cancelled per Privilege Club rules. Avios redeposited to account; cancellation fee applies for non-Privilege Club Silver/Gold members (approximately QAR 150 per person). Cancel at least 24 hours before departure. Taxes and fees refunded to original payment.
claimSteps: Step 1: Go to qatarairways.com and log in to your Privilege Club account
Step 2: Navigate to 'Manage Booking' and enter booking reference + last name
Step 3: Select your flight and review the cancellation and change options
Step 4: For Economy Classic: choose to change to the lower-priced flight, paying any applicable change fee
Step 5: For Economy Flex or Business refundable fares: cancel and request full refund, then rebook at lower fare
Step 6: For refund requests: complete the refund form at qatarairways.com/refund
Step 7: Refunds processed within 7-20 business days depending on original payment method
policies.creditExpiry: Qatar Airways travel credit (where issued): typically valid 12 months from issue date. Privilege Club Avios: no expiry as long as account shows activity within 36 months (one Avios earn or redeem transaction required).

cancellationFees.economyLite: 100% forfeiture after 24-hour window — no refund or credit
cancellationFees.economyClassic: Change fee approximately $100-250 USD depending on route and timing; remaining value credited
cancellationFees.economyFlex: $0 cancellation fee — full refund to original payment
cancellationFees.business: Varies by sub-class — non-refundable Business Light exists; fully refundable Business Flex
cancellationFees.aviosRedeposit: ~QAR 150 (~$40 USD) per person for non-Silver/Gold Privilege Club members

refundTypes.cash: Economy Flex and refundable Business fares; EU261-mandated refunds for cancelled flights
refundTypes.travelCredit: Economy Classic cancellations (after change fee deduction); valid 12 months
refundTypes.avios: Privilege Club award cancellations — Avios redeposited (fee applies for non-elite)

loopholes: EU261 applies if your Qatar itinerary includes a leg originating in the EU (e.g., connecting through Frankfurt, Paris, or Amsterdam) — entitled to €250-600 compensation for delays/cancellations of 3+ hours
If Qatar cancels or significantly changes your flight (5+ hours), you're entitled to a full cash refund on any fare type — do not accept credits without requesting cash first
Qatar Airways has a 'cooling-off' period for US-origin bookings — use this aggressively if you spot a lower fare within 24 hours
Qatar's award chart is distance-based — cancelling and rebooking at a lower Avios rate can yield significant savings on peak routes
Privilege Club Silver/Gold members get enhanced change/cancel rights and reduced redeposit fees — worth checking status thresholds before booking non-refundable fares
Qatar regularly offers upgrade bids and fare promotions — monitor email marketing after booking for discounted upgrades

policies.priceDropPolicy: Singapore Airlines does not offer a formal price-drop refund or price-match guarantee. Strategy depends on fare class: Economy Lite: non-refundable, non-changeable after booking; US 24-hour window is the only exception. Economy Standard: changes with a fee; cancel and rebook at lower fare. Economy Flexi: fully changeable and refundable. Business Class Standard/Flexi: similar tiered flexibility.
policies.twentyFourHourRule: For tickets originating in the United States (SQ operates nonstop to SFO, LAX, JFK, and HNL), Singapore Airlines complies with the US DOT 24-hour cancellation rule: full refund to original payment if cancelled within 24 hours of booking, provided departure is at least 7 days away. Process via Manage Booking at singaporeair.com or by phone.
policies.basicEconomyRules: Economy Lite — Singapore Airlines' most restrictive fare: carry-on bag only (no checked baggage without surcharge), seat selection not included at booking, no changes permitted after purchase (outside 24h US DOT window), fully non-refundable. Full forfeiture upon cancellation. No upgrade eligibility. Book Economy Standard or Flexi for any required flexibility.
policies.awardTicketPolicy: KrisFlyer award tickets: miles refunded to KrisFlyer account upon cancellation. Redeposit fee: approximately USD $37.50 per person for non-KrisFlyer Elite/Elite Gold members. KrisFlyer Elite Silver and Elite Gold: waived redeposit fees. Taxes and fuel surcharges refunded to original payment within 14 business days.
claimSteps: Step 1: Log in to singaporeair.com using your KrisFlyer account or booking reference
Step 2: Navigate to 'Manage Booking' and select your flight
Step 3: Review fare conditions for your specific booking class
Step 4: For Economy Standard: select 'Change Flight' and pay applicable change fee to rebook at lower fare
Step 5: For Economy Flexi or Business Flexi: cancel for full refund, then rebook at new lower price
Step 6: Submit refund request through singaporeair.com/manage-booking or contact reservations
Step 7: Refund processed within 14 business days to original payment method
policies.creditExpiry: Singapore Airlines travel credits: typically valid 12 months from issue date. KrisFlyer miles: valid as long as any earning or redemption activity occurs within 36 months. Inactive accounts may have miles expired.

cancellationFees.economyLite: 100% forfeiture after 24-hour window — no refund or credit issued
cancellationFees.economyStandard: Change fee approximately $150-300 USD depending on route and timing; remaining value as travel credit
cancellationFees.economyFlexi: $0 cancellation fee — full refund to original payment
cancellationFees.businessStandard: Change fee applicable — varies by route; remaining value credited
cancellationFees.businessFlexi: $0 cancellation fee — full refund to original payment
cancellationFees.krisFlyer: ~$37.50 USD per person for non-Elite; waived for Elite Silver and Elite Gold

refundTypes.cash: Economy Flexi and Business Flexi fares; also when SQ cancels or significantly changes your flight
refundTypes.travelCredit: Economy Standard cancellations (after change fee deduction); valid 12 months
refundTypes.miles: KrisFlyer award ticket cancellations — miles redeposited (fee applies for non-elite members)

loopholes: Singapore Airlines has a strong reputation for customer service — polite calls to reservations have sometimes resulted in fee waivers or goodwill credits, particularly for long-term KrisFlyer members
If SQ significantly alters your itinerary (schedule change of 2+ hours), you can request a full cash refund on any fare type including Economy Lite
KrisFlyer Solitaire PPS Club and PPS Club members get enhanced waiver rights — check status before assuming full change fees apply
Singapore Airlines' partnership with Star Alliance means significant schedule changes by a codeshare partner may entitle you to SQ refund rights
Book via singaporeair.com directly — OTA bookings significantly complicate change/cancel requests and may not qualify for direct airline protections
Economy Standard change fees can be partially offset if the price drop savings exceed the fee — always calculate net savings before deciding

policies.priceDropPolicy: Cathay Pacific (rebranded as Cathay in 2023, still operating as Cathay Pacific) does not offer a formal price-drop refund or price-match guarantee. Strategy depends on fare class: Economy Light: non-refundable, non-changeable — full forfeiture except US 24-hour window. Economy Standard: changes with fee. Economy Flex: fully changeable and refundable. Premium Economy and Business: tiered flexibility by sub-class.
policies.twentyFourHourRule: For US-originating tickets, Cathay Pacific complies with the US DOT 24-hour cancellation rule: full refund within 24 hours of booking if departure is at least 7 days away. Cathay Pacific operates nonstop to JFK and LAX from Hong Kong, with connections through Vancouver (YVR) and Toronto (YYZ). Process cancellation via Manage Booking at cathaypacific.com.
policies.basicEconomyRules: Economy Light — Cathay Pacific's most restrictive economy fare: carry-on bag only (no checked baggage), seat assignment at check-in (not selectable at booking), no changes or cancellations after 24-hour US DOT window, fully non-refundable. Full ticket value forfeited upon cancellation. No upgrade eligibility. Book Economy Standard or Flex for any required flexibility.
policies.awardTicketPolicy: Asia Miles (now Cathay Miles) award tickets: miles refunded to Cathay membership account upon cancellation. Redeposit fee: approximately USD $37 per person for standard members; waived or reduced for Marco Polo Club and Cathay membership tier holders. Taxes and fees refunded to original payment within 7-14 business days.
claimSteps: Step 1: Go to cathaypacific.com and log in or use Manage Booking with booking reference + last name
Step 2: Navigate to your booking and review fare conditions for your ticket class
Step 3: For Economy Standard: select 'Change Flight' and pay the applicable change fee to rebook at the lower fare
Step 4: For Economy Flex or refundable Business: cancel for full refund, then rebook at the new lower price
Step 5: Request refund at cathaypacific.com/refund or via the app
Step 6: Refund processed within 7-14 business days to original payment method
Step 7: For Cathay Miles award refunds: contact Cathay membership services
policies.creditExpiry: Cathay Pacific travel credits: valid 12 months from issue date. Cathay Miles (Asia Miles): valid 18 months from last account activity (earn or redeem).

cancellationFees.economyLight: 100% forfeiture after 24-hour window — no refund or credit
cancellationFees.economyStandard: Change fee approximately $150-300 USD depending on route; remaining value as travel credit
cancellationFees.economyFlex: $0 cancellation fee — full refund to original payment
cancellationFees.premiumEconomy: Standard sub-class: change fee applies; Flex: $0, full refund
cancellationFees.business: Standard sub-class: change fee applies; Flex: $0, fully refundable
cancellationFees.milesRedeposit: ~$37 USD per person for standard members; waived for Marco Polo Diamond/Diamond Plus

refundTypes.cash: Economy Flex and Business Flex fares; mandated by DOT/airline when Cathay cancels your flight
refundTypes.travelCredit: Economy Standard cancellations (after change fee); valid 12 months
refundTypes.miles: Cathay Miles award cancellations — miles redeposited (fee applies for non-elite)

loopholes: If Cathay Pacific cancels your flight or significantly changes your schedule (3+ hours), you are entitled to a full cash refund on any fare class — do not accept credits without asking for cash
Cathay Pacific / Cathay app sometimes shows lower fares within 24h of booking — always check before the DOT window closes
Marco Polo Club Diamond and Diamond Plus members get enhanced change/cancel rights and waived redeposit fees — check status before booking Economy Light
Cathay is part of oneworld alliance — schedule changes by AA, JAL, or other partners on connecting itineraries may trigger Cathay refund rights
Asia Miles / Cathay Miles: the 18-month activity requirement is strict — set a reminder if you're holding miles and not planning to fly soon
Book directly at cathaypacific.com to retain full change/cancel rights — OTA bookings may not qualify for direct airline processing

policies.priceDropPolicy: Air Canada does not offer a formal price-drop refund or price-match guarantee. Strategy by fare class: Basic: no changes or cancellations after 24-hour window — full forfeiture. Standard: changes with a fee. Flex: changes free, refund as travel credit. Comfort/Latitude: more flexible. Business/Signature: fully refundable. Cancel and rebook is the primary strategy for non-refundable fares.
policies.twentyFourHourRule: Air Canada complies with the Canadian Air Passenger Protection Regulations (APPR) and US DOT 24-hour rule for US-originating tickets. Full refund within 24 hours of booking for most fare types if departure is at least 7 days away (US rule). In Canada: APPR provides broad passenger protections including mandatory refunds for airline-caused cancellations. Cancel via aircanada.com or by calling Air Canada reservations.
policies.basicEconomyRules: Basic fare — Air Canada's most restrictive fare: carry-on bag included but no checked baggage, seat assignment at check-in, non-changeable and non-refundable after 24-hour window, no upgrade eligibility, no Aeroplan miles earn on most Basic tickets, cannot select seat in advance. Full forfeiture upon cancellation after 24-hour period. Book Standard or higher for any flexibility.
policies.awardTicketPolicy: Aeroplan award tickets: points refunded to Aeroplan account upon cancellation. Cancellation fee: $150 CAD (~$110 USD) for non-Elite status members; fee waived for Aeroplan 35K, 50K, 75K, and Super Elite members. Cancel at least 24 hours before departure. Taxes and fees refunded to original payment within 7-14 business days.
claimSteps: Step 1: Log in to aircanada.com and navigate to 'Manage My Bookings'
Step 2: Select the booking with the price drop
Step 3: Review your fare type — Basic, Standard, Flex, Comfort, Latitude, Business, or Signature
Step 4: For Flex or higher: select 'Cancel Flight' — travel credit or cash refund issued depending on fare
Step 5: For Standard: pay applicable change fee to rebook at the lower price
Step 6: Search for the same route at the new lower fare
Step 7: Apply travel credit or Aeroplan points to new booking at checkout
policies.creditExpiry: Air Canada travel credits: typically valid 24 months from issue date (more generous than US carriers). Aeroplan points: valid as long as account shows activity within 12 months (earn or redeem). Inactive accounts expire points — set a recurring small earn to keep account active.

cancellationFees.basic: 100% forfeiture after 24-hour window — no credit or refund
cancellationFees.standard: Change fee CAD $100-300 depending on route; remaining value as travel credit
cancellationFees.flex: $0 change fee — remaining value as non-refundable travel credit; valid 24 months
cancellationFees.comfort: $0 change fee — travel credit or partial cash refund depending on original payment
cancellationFees.latitude: $0 change fee — full refund to original payment
cancellationFees.business: $0 cancellation fee — full refund to original payment (Latitude Business and above)
cancellationFees.aeroplanCancel: CAD $150 (~$110 USD) per person for non-Elite; waived for 35K status and above

refundTypes.cash: Latitude, Business, and Signature fares; mandated by APPR when Air Canada cancels your flight
refundTypes.travelCredit: Flex and Comfort cancellations; valid 24 months from issue (generous vs. US carriers)
refundTypes.aeroplan: Aeroplan award cancellations — points redeposited (fee applies for non-Elite)

loopholes: Canadian APPR: If Air Canada cancels your flight or causes a delay of 3+ hours for reasons within airline control, you're entitled to cash compensation ($125-$1,000 CAD) AND a refund — stronger than US DOT rules
Air Canada's travel credits are valid 24 months — significantly more time than US carriers' 12-month window; no rush to rebook immediately
Flex fare: zero change fees — always rebook at a lower fare if the price drops, even if only marginally
Aeroplan Elite members (35K+) get completely free award cancellations — no redeposit fee, making Aeroplan very flexible for status holders
Air Canada Credit Card (TD Aeroplan Visa, AMEX Aeroplan): may include trip protection or change fee waivers — check card benefits before paying fees
Air Canada regularly runs seat sales (Latitude and up) — if you booked Standard and a Latitude sale appears at a similar price, upgrade may be worth it for full refund flexibility
